title: Dispute
description: Bonded challenge against a settlement's order line(s) (§5.1, §6.4). Per-unit: never blocks other buyers. Prose is inadmissible; evidence is machine evidence only.

unit_refs.description: The specific order line(s) this dispute concerns, by buyer_principal_hash.

challenger_principal_id.description: Revealed only post-ruling per §6.7 (challenger identity is not exposed while the dispute is open).

bond.description: USDC 6-decimal base units, string-encoded integer. params.disputes.challengeBond = max(1% of disputed value, $100).

reason_code.description: Enumerated codes only; free-text reason is inadmissible by contract (§6.4). This closed set is an implementation-level enumeration of the failure modes named across §6.2-6.4; the plan mandates enumeration but does not itself list literal codes.

evidence.description: Machine evidence hashes only (signed API responses, zkTLS transcripts, oracle pull records). May be empty for an evidence-free filing, which is precisely the case that suffers 100% bond forfeiture (§6.4).

encrypted_evidence_ref.description: URI to the full evidence package, encrypted off-chain.

panel.description: Null until/unless the dispute survives predicate re-runs and escalates to the 3-operator verifier panel (§6.4).

members.description: 3 independent, staked, KYB'd operators from different organizations.

ruling_predicate_trace.description: Reference (hash or URI) to the deterministic predicate re-run trace; any staker can re-run it (§6.4).

appeal.description: Null unless the panel ruling is appealed. 5-of-7 fresh operators, doubled bond, final (§6.4).

bond_2x.description: True when the doubled appeal bond has been posted.

slashes.description: Slash events triggered by the ruling, feeding the §6.5 remedy waterfall.
